channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: On-Device Function Calling in Google AI Edge Gallery
item.description: Google has launched FunctionGemma, a 270M parameter model that enables on-device function calling for efficient AI experiences on mobile devices. This allows users to perform complex tasks offline with low latency, enhancing mobile AI capabilities.

item.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
item.description: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now features a new ecosystem of third-party integrations, allowing developers to connect their AI agents with tools like GitHub and Notion. This expansion aims to facilitate the creation of more capable real-world applications.

item.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
item.description: Google I/O 2026 is scheduled for May 19-20, featuring a save the date puzzle that showcases AI's potential. This annual tradition helps build excitement for the event while highlighting AI's role in enhancing experiences.

item.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
item.description: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas will offer an in-person experience focused on networking and hands-on problem solving. The event will cover various topics related to agentic AI and provide developers with essential tools.

item.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
item.description: Google has launched LiteRT, the successor to TFLite, which provides faster GPU and NPU acceleration and better support for PyTorch and JAX. This update enhances efficiency and security within the TensorFlow ecosystem.

item.title: Introducing Wednesday Build Hour
item.description: Wednesday Build Hour is a new interactive session by Google Cloud aimed at helping developers enhance their cloud skills through hands-on building. It focuses on advanced topics, providing immediate practical results.

item.title: Unleash Your Development Superpowers: Refining the Core Coding Experience
item.description: The Gemini Code Assist team has rolled out updates to improve the coding experience, introducing tools like Agent Mode and Inline Diff Views. These features aim to customize AI assistance to better fit individual developer workflows.

item.title: Anthropic says Claude Code subscribers will need to pay extra for OpenClaw usage
item.description: Claude Code subscribers will face additional charges for using OpenClaw and other third-party tools, increasing the cost of accessing Anthropic's coding assistant. This change may impact users' budgeting for AI tools.

item.title: A folk musician became a target for AI fakes and a copyright troll
item.description: Folk musician Murphy Campbell found unauthorized AI-generated versions of her songs on Spotify, leading to concerns about copyright infringement and the authenticity of music online. This incident highlights the challenges artists face in the age of AI.

item.title: Really, you made this without AI? Prove it
item.description: As generative AI technology advances, creators face skepticism regarding the authenticity of their work. The increasing ability of AI to mimic human creations raises questions about originality and trust in creative fields.

item.title: Anthropic is having a moment in the private markets; SpaceX could spoil the party
item.description: Anthropic is currently the most sought-after private market investment, while OpenAI is losing traction. SpaceX's upcoming IPO could significantly impact this landscape.

item.title: Extra usage credit for Claude to celebrate usage bundles launch (Pro, Max, Team)
item.description: Claude is offering extra usage credits to celebrate the launch of its new usage bundles for Pro, Max, and Team plans.

item.title: Supply Chain Attacks Surge in March 2026
item.description: March 2026 saw a notable rise in software supply chain attacks, including a significant compromise of the Axios NPM package linked to North Korean hackers.

item.title: pluck-mcp 0.1.1
item.description: Pluck has released version 0.1.1 of its MCP server, enabling users to query their knowledge graph from various clients.

item.title: Show HN: SpeechSDK – free, open-source SDK that unifies all AI voice models
item.description: SpeechSDK is a new open-source SDK that integrates various AI voice models into a unified platform.

item.title: Tell HN: Anthropic no longer allowing Claude Code subscriptions to use OpenClaw
item.description: Anthropic has announced that Claude Code subscriptions will no longer support third-party harnesses like OpenClaw starting April 4.

item.title: air-controls 0.1.0
item.description: Version 0.1.0 of air-controls has been released, providing runtime visibility and audit trails for AI agents across various platforms.

item.title: air-controls added to PyPI
item.description: The air-controls package has been added to PyPI, offering tools for monitoring AI agents' activities.

item.title: skill-installer 0.1.2
item.description: Skill-installer version 0.1.2 has been released, allowing users to install Claude Code skills directly from GitHub.

item.title: pluck-mcp added to PyPI
item.description: Pluck-mcp has been added to PyPI, enabling users to query their knowledge graph from various clients.

item.title: glassbox-mech-interp 4.2.0
item.description: The release of glassbox-mech-interp 4.2.0 focuses on mechanistic interpretability and compliance with the EU AI Act, introducing various frameworks for AI analysis.

item.title: Deepfakes and malware: AI menu grows longer for threat actors, causing headaches for defenders
item.description: AI technologies are increasingly being weaponized by malicious actors, leading to a rise in threats like deepfakes and malware, creating challenges for cybersecurity.

item.title: hermes-px 0.0.4
item.description: Hermes-px 0.0.4 is introduced as a secure AI inference proxy library that utilizes Tor routing, serving as a drop-in replacement for the OpenAI SDK.

item.title: Two OpenAI Execs, Including CEO of AGI, Going on Medical Leave
item.description: Two executives from OpenAI, including the CEO of AGI, are going on medical leave, raising questions about the company's leadership during critical times.

item.title: hermes-px 0.0.3
item.description: Hermes-px 0.0.3 is another version of the secure AI inference proxy library with Tor routing, continuing the trend of enhancing AI security.

item.title: After Cutting Down on 'Side Quests,' OpenAI Bought a Talk Show
item.description: OpenAI has decided to discontinue its Sora image generator and delay the launch of a planned adult mode for ChatGPT, focusing on core projects.

item.title: Microsoft Teams: Sharing recap access
item.description: Microsoft Teams has introduced a feature allowing recording and transcript owners to grant access to specific individuals without requests, enhancing user experience.

item.title: Chuck Norris' Family Warns of AI-Generated Videos Spreading 'Fabricated Reports' About His Health Before Death
item.description: Chuck Norris' family has issued a warning about AI-generated videos spreading false reports about his health, highlighting the dangers of misinformation.

item.title: llama-cloud 2.2.0
item.description: The llama-cloud 2.2.0 release is the official Python library for accessing the llama-cloud API, facilitating easier integration for developers.

item.title: llama-agents-core 0.8.4
item.description: Llama-agents-core 0.8.4 introduces core models and schemas for LlamaDeploy, enhancing the functionality of AI deployment.

item.title: llama-agents-appserver 0.10.2
item.description: LlamaDeploy has released version 0.10.2 of its application server components, enhancing its deployment capabilities. This update is significant for developers using LlamaDeploy.

item.title: llama-agents-agentcore 0.8.7
item.description: Version 0.8.7 of LlamaAgents' AgentCore deployment utilities has been launched, aimed at improving integration with Bedrock. This update is relevant for developers in AI deployment.

item.title: openinference-instrumentation-langchain 0.1.62
item.description: OpenInference has introduced version 0.1.62 of its LangChain Instrumentation, which aids in monitoring and optimizing AI models. This is crucial for developers focused on performance.

item.title: Developer behind controversial AI apps sues Apple over App Store takedowns
item.description: The developer behind controversial AI apps, Ex-Human, is suing Apple for removing its apps from the App Store, claiming the actions were arbitrary. This lawsuit highlights ongoing tensions between developers and platform providers.

item.title: bernstein 1.4.13
item.description: Bernstein has released version 1.4.13, focusing on declarative agent orchestration for engineering teams. This update aims to streamline workflows in AI project management.

item.title: azure-ai-evaluation 1.16.4
item.description: Microsoft has launched version 1.16.4 of its Azure Evaluation Library for Python, providing tools for evaluating AI models. This is significant for developers working with Azure AI services.

item.title: Meta Pauses Work With Mercor After Data Breach Puts AI Industry Secrets at Risk
item.description: Meta has paused its collaboration with Mercor following a data breach that may have compromised sensitive AI training data. This incident raises concerns about data security in the AI industry.

item.title: Charles Schwab Is Gearing Up to Offer Bitcoin, Ethereum Spot Trading
item.description: Charles Schwab plans to offer spot trading for Bitcoin and Ethereum by the end of the quarter, marking a significant move into cryptocurrency trading. This could impact the financial landscape.

item.title: Off-Grid Data Centers Will Help Protect Ratepayers, But Codifying ARC-ES Would Do Even More
item.description: The article discusses the importance of off-grid data centers for energy security in the U.S., especially as the nation approaches its 250th birthday. It emphasizes the need for sustainable energy solutions.

item.title: langchain-swarmsync added to PyPI
item.description: LangChain has added SwarmSync integration to PyPI, enabling agent-to-agent marketplace functionalities and intelligent LLM routing. This enhances collaboration in AI applications.

item.title: ioc-cfn-cognition-engines added to PyPI
item.description: The IOC CFN Cognition Engine has been added to PyPI, offering tools for knowledge extraction and semantic negotiation. This addition enhances the resources available for developers in AI applications.

item.title: Three insights you may have missed from theCUBE’s coverage of RSAC 2026
item.description: At RSAC 2026, the theme 'The Power of Community' highlighted the cybersecurity sector's growing autonomy and vulnerability to AI attacks. Insights from discussions emphasized the need for collaboration in addressing these challenges.

item.title: Netflix - yes Netflix - jumps on the AI bandwagon with video editor
item.description: Netflix has introduced a new AI video editor that alters object interactions in scenes, potentially transforming filmmaking. This innovation aims to streamline the editing process and enhance creative possibilities.

item.title: bernstein 1.4.12
item.description: The release of Bernstein 1.4.12 focuses on declarative agent orchestration, aimed at improving workflows for engineering teams. This update is significant for teams looking to enhance their project management.

item.title: OpenAI executive shuffle includes new role for COO Brad Lightcap to lead ‘special projects’
item.description: OpenAI has shuffled its executive team, appointing COO Brad Lightcap to lead special projects while CMO Kate Rouch steps back for health reasons. This change may influence the company's strategic direction.

item.title: Anthropic buys biotech startup Coefficient Bio in $400M deal: reports | TechCrunch
item.description: Anthropic has acquired biotech startup Coefficient Bio for $400 million, enhancing its capabilities in AI applications for the medical field. This move reflects a growing trend of AI companies diversifying into healthcare.

item.title: OpenClaw gives users yet another reason to be freaked out about security
item.description: The OpenClaw tool has raised security concerns by allowing attackers to gain unauthorized admin access. This incident underscores the risks associated with AI tools in cybersecurity.

item.title: Anthropic buys biotech startup Coefficient Bio in $400M deal: Reports
item.description: Anthropic's acquisition of Coefficient Bio for $400 million has been reported, emphasizing its strategic investment in biotech. This acquisition is part of a broader trend of AI firms expanding into healthcare.

item.title: Anthropic reportedly acquires medical AI startup Coefficient Bio for $400M+
item.description: Anthropic has reportedly acquired Coefficient Bio, a medical AI startup, for over $400 million. This acquisition is significant for enhancing AI's role in medical research.

item.title: Anthropic ramps up its political activities with a new PAC
item.description: Anthropic has launched a new political action committee (PAC) to support candidates aligned with its AI policy agenda ahead of the midterm elections. This move indicates the company's growing involvement in political advocacy.
